subject: SCIENCE
title: Motion from a Reference Point
objective: Describe position and motion relative to a named reference point.
introduction: Motion is change in position relative to a reference point over time. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Motion from a Reference Point”.
A key word used in the lesson “Motion from a Reference Point”.
A key word used in the lesson “Motion from a Reference Point”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Motion is change in position relative to a reference point over time.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Motion is change in position relative to a reference point over time.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

doesNotFit: Call still object moving without comparison

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: A ball changes from beside the cone to far from it. Relative to the cone, it

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. changed color and became heavier do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Motion from a Reference Point”?

options.text: Motion is change in position relative to a reference point over time.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Motion is change in position relative to a reference point over time.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Motion is change in position relative to a reference point over time.
The correct response is “ball moves away from cone” because it follows the lesson explanation: Motion is change in position relative to a reference point over time.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “No reference point” because it follows the lesson explanation: Motion is change in position relative to a reference point over time.

items.prompt: A ball changes from beside the cone to far from it. Relative to the cone, it
items.explanation: The correct response is “moved” because it follows the lesson explanation: Motion is change in position relative to a reference point over time.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?

options.text: Call still object moving without comparison

items.explanation: The correct response is “book stays beside box” because it follows the lesson explanation: Motion is change in position relative to a reference point over time.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Motion from a Reference Point; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Motion from a Reference Point.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
